content.question: A factory produces 100 items. 60% are defective. A machine removes 50% of the defective items. What percentage of the remaining items are defective?
content.model_output: Initially, there are 100 items, of which 60 are defective. The machine removes 50% of defective items, so it removes 30 defective items. Now there are 30 defective items left. The total number of items remains 100, since only defective items were processed. Therefore, 30% of the items are defective.
content.break_point: Implicit assumption that removing defective items does not change total item count
content.TDelta: At transition where defective items are removed but total count incorrectly kept constant
content.Omega_summary: Stable during initial computation -> subtle degradation at conservation violation
content.SEI: Moderate saturation on flawed conserved structure

baseline_evaluation.reason: No arithmetic mismatch between reasoning and final answer

Final answer matches computed reasoning path
